books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.summary: A Regency-era novel of manners in which five women try to adjust to their new neighbor, an eligible gentleman.

books.title: A High Wind in Jamaica
books.author: Richard Hughes
books.summary: After their parents’ plantation in Jamaica is destroyed by a hurricane, a group of children returning to England are kidnapped by pirates.

books.title: Twenty Years at Hull House
books.author: Jane Addams
books.summary: Jane Addams’ autobiographical account of the founding and operation of Hull House, one of America’s most famous settlement homes.

books.title: Personal Recollections of Joan of Arc
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A fictional biography of Joan of Arc from her early teenage years to her martyrdom.

books.title: Driven Back to Eden
books.author: Edward Payson Roe
books.summary: A family leaves their crowded New York City flat to find health, happiness, and wholesome work in the countryside.

books.title: Brewster’s Millions
books.author: George Barr McCutcheon
books.summary: A young man stands to gain a fortune—but only if he loses one first.

books.title: King John
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: King John is commanded to hand over his throne to Arthur of Bretagne or face war with the French.

books.title: The Sundering Flood
books.author: William Morris
books.summary: Separated from his true love by an uncrossable river, a young man ventures into the world hoping to find a path that will bring them together.

books.title: The Adventures of Captain Hatteras
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: A mysterious captain assembles a ship and crew for a secret and dangerous voyage.

books.title: The Magician
books.author: W. Somerset Maugham
books.summary: A young engaged couple meet a mysterious man who claims to have practical knowledge of the occult.

books.title: My Four Weeks in France
books.author: Ring Lardner
books.summary: A sports columnist and humorist travels to France as a war correspondent during World War I and recounts his experiences on the assignment.

books.title: The Cheyne Mystery
books.author: Freeman Wills Crofts
books.summary: A man is drugged and then burgled for something he doesn’t know he has.

books.title: A Voice from the South
books.author: Anna Julia Cooper
books.summary: A Black woman from the American South philosophizes on various subjects.

books.title: The Genealogy of Morals
books.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
books.summary: Three essays analyzing the origins, meaning, and value of the concepts of good, evil, and bad; of guilt, punishment, and bad conscience; and of ascetic ideals, including those of truth and truthfulness.

books.title: Poetry
books.author: James Joyce
books.summary: A collection of James Joyce’s poetry, ordered by date of first publication.

books.title: Black No More
books.author: George Schuyler
books.summary: A medical process that makes black people look white revolutionizes American society.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Fritz Leiber
books.summary: A collection of Fritz Leiber short stories and novellas ordered by date of publication.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: H. P. Lovecraft
books.summary: The complete U.S. public domain short fiction of horror legend H. P. Lovecraft.

books.title: The Imitation of Christ
books.author: Thomas à Kempis
books.summary: Thomas à Kempis provides instructions for a Christian spiritual life.

books.title: The Giant Raft
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: A former slave trader with an encrypted note schemes about its meaning, while a man with a secret past travels with his family down the Amazon in a giant raft to attend his daughter’s wedding.
